figures.caption: Fig. 2. Task success rate vs. evolution iteration for all five methods. Capability Evolution (ours, solid blue) shows sustained improvement across 20 iterations, reaching 91.3%. Agent Modification (dashed red) and SkiMo (dash-dotted teal) achieve moderate gains. SPiRL (dotted olive) levels off as frozen skills cannot adapt. Static ECM (dotted gray) plateaus early at 41.2%. Shaded region indicates ±1 standard deviation for our method.

Fig. 1. Capability evolution loop for embodied agents. A persistent agent (blue, top) maintains its identity and decision-making role, while capabilities (ECMs) evolve over time through iterative execution, experience collection, and learning. A runtime layer (purple) enforces policy constraints and manages execution, ensuring that capability evolution remains safe and controllable.
TABLE VII LEARNING MODALITY ABLATION. EACH ROW USES ONLY THE INDICATED MODALITY (OR COMBINATION) FOR THE UPDATE FUNCTION. ↑80% INDICATES THE EARLIEST ITERATION REACHING 80% SUCCESS.
